function_overview.content: Provides full lifecycle management of the NMD product canonical entity table — creating, updating, deactivating, and managing the status of instrument master records, with a full audit trail on every write.

permissions: Change status to any value subject to parent status constraint (BR-006)
Set status as defined by source configuration only

preconditions.description: Canonical entity table is initialised and accessible
Phase 1 asset class definitions (Futures & Options, FX) are configured in the system
Hash algorithm is agreed and implemented platform-wide (see OI-001 in FRD-NMD-0001)
For Data Owner path: user is authenticated with Data Owner role
For Source Processing path: source configuration exists with golden source flag and initial status defined (FRD-NMD-0002)

dependencies.description: FRD-NMD-0002 source configuration schema — auto-create path cannot be designed until source configuration is finalised
Asset class mapping definitions for Phase 1 (Futures & Options, FX) — field validation rules for entity type and Product Type cannot be finalised without these
Hash algorithm agreed across all NMD modules — canonical key computation must be consistent platform-wide

entity_definition.name: Canonical Record
entity_definition.description: A single row in the canonical entity table representing one unique financial instrument, identified by its canonical key. No hard delete — retirement via Disable status only.

field_catalog.notes: Deterministic hash; same 5-column input always produces the same key
Mandatory on every record. For FX records: fixed value 'FX'

field_catalog.create: Optional — required for entity types above L1
field_catalog.notes: For FX Currency Pair: must be XXXYYY format (6 uppercase characters)

field_catalog.create: Optional — required for L3+ entity types
field_catalog.notes: Futures & Options: PHY | FUT | CMB | OOP | OOF | OOC. FX: must be null

field_catalog.create: Optional — required for L3+ entity types
Optional — required for contract-level entity types
field_catalog.notes: Format validated per entity type. Option Contract: YYYYMM_CP_STRIKE | YYYYMMDD_CP_STRIKE | YYYYMMWN_CP_STRIKE where CP = C or P. FX: must be null
Must match a predefined entity type for the selected asset class
Active | Inactive | Disable. Subject to parent status constraint (BR-006). Source Processing: from source configuration only
Identifies creation actor: 'User' or source processing identifier

detail_view.fields: All fields including created_by, created_at, source, and link to audit trail
detail_view.audit_trail: Full immutable audit log accessible from detail view

crud_operations.description: Create a new canonical record. Canonical key computed from 5 hierarchy columns. Initial status set by actor: user-selected for Data Owner, source configuration for Source Processing.
Update non-locked fields on an existing canonical record. The 5 hierarchy columns, asset_class, entity_type, and canonical_key are immutable after creation.
post_conditions: Audit log entry created for each changed field

crud_operations.description: Change the status of a canonical record. Triggers top-down cascade to all non-Disabled descendants.
post_conditions: Audit log entry created for each affected record

crud_operations.description: Not permitted. Retirement via Disable status only.

rules.rule: Exchange is mandatory on every record — creation rejected without it
Canonical key is computed as a deterministic hash of all 5 columns (null placeholders for skipped levels) at creation and is immutable thereafter

The 5 hierarchy columns (exchange, commodity, product_type, product_code, contract), asset_class, and entity_type are locked after creation — corrections require deactivating the record and creating a new one
Entity type must match a predefined type for the selected asset class; column population must conform to that entity type's rules
Status cascade is top-down — a status change propagates to all non-Disabled descendants; Disabled descendants are exempt and remain unchanged
A child record's status cannot be more permissive than its parent: child cannot be Active if parent is Inactive or Disabled; child cannot be Inactive if parent is Disabled
Data Owner may set status to any value subject to BR-006; Source Processing may only set status as defined by source configuration
Source Processing initial status is taken from source configuration — never defaulted to Active
Duplicate records (same canonical key hash) are rejected on creation
All writes produce an immutable audit log entry — no write path bypasses the audit trail
Must be one of: Futures & Options, FX
Must match a predefined entity type for the selected asset_class
rules.error_message: Entity type not valid for asset class

rules.rule: For Futures & Options: must be one of PHY, FUT, CMB, OOP, OOF, OOC. For FX: must be null
rules.error_message: Invalid product type for asset class

rules.rule: For Option Contract entity type: must match YYYYMM_CP_STRIKE | YYYYMMDD_CP_STRIKE | YYYYMMWN_CP_STRIKE where CP = C or P. For FX: must be null
rules.error_message: Invalid contract format for entity type

rules.rule: For FX Currency Pair entity type: must be exactly 6 uppercase characters (XXXYYY format)
rules.error_message: Currency pair must be 6 uppercase characters

rules.rule: Must be unique across all records
Child status cannot be more permissive than parent status

criteria.criterion: Given a Data Owner submits a valid new canonical record with all required fields, When the create operation executes, Then the record is persisted with a computed canonical key and an audit log entry is created
Given the same 5 hierarchy column values are submitted twice, When canonical key is computed, Then both produce the identical hash
Given a create is attempted without an Exchange value, When validation runs, Then the request is rejected with 'Exchange is required'
Given a create is attempted with a duplicate canonical key, When the system checks uniqueness, Then the request is rejected with 'Duplicate canonical record'
Given a Data Owner changes the status of a parent record, When the change is saved, Then all non-Disabled descendants are updated to the new status and audit entries are created for each
Given a Data Owner attempts to set a child record to Active when the parent is Inactive or Disabled, When validation runs, Then the request is rejected with the parent status constraint error
Given Source Processing creates a canonical record from a golden source, When the record is persisted, Then initial status is taken from source configuration — never defaulted to Active
Given an entity type of Option Contract is submitted, When contract format is validated, Then only YYYYMM_CP_STRIKE, YYYYMMDD_CP_STRIKE, or YYYYMMWN_CP_STRIKE formats are accepted
Given a Futures & Options record is submitted with a Product Type not in PHY/FUT/CMB/OOP/OOF/OOC, When validation runs, Then the request is rejected with permitted values listed
Given an FX Currency Pair record is submitted with a Commodity not matching XXXYYY format, When validation runs, Then the request is rejected with 'Currency pair must be 6 uppercase characters'

Given any canonical write occurs (create, update, status change), When the operation completes, Then an immutable audit log entry exists capturing actor, timestamp, field changed, old value, and new value
Given a Data Owner attempts to update any of the 5 hierarchy columns on an existing record, When the update is submitted, Then the request is rejected — locked fields cannot be modified

assumptions.description: Asset class column mappings for Phase 1 (Futures & Options, FX) are defined before detailed design begins
Hash algorithm is agreed platform-wide before implementation
Source Processing (FRD-NMD-0002) owns and provides the golden source flag and initial status configuration
No bulk import required in Phase 1 — records created individually by Data Owner or Source Processing

constraints.description: Phase 1 asset classes: Futures & Options and FX only
No hard deletes — retirement via Disable status only
The 5 hierarchy columns and canonical key are immutable after creation — corrections require deactivate + new record
Write access restricted to Data Owner and Source Processing only

questions.question: Is there a formal correction workflow for locked-field errors (e.g., wrong exchange on creation), or is deactivate + new record the only path?
What is the SLA for status cascade on large parent records (e.g., disabling an Exchange with thousands of descendants)? Flagged in NFR-NMD-001-002 but not yet quantified.

entries.author: Leo
entries.description: Initial draft — DATA_PAGE spec for FA-01 Canonical Maintenance from FRD-NMD-0001. Covers field catalog, CRUD operations, business rules, validation rules, and acceptance criteria for both Futures & Options and FX asset classes.
